releases.title: collectZ v3.15.0
releases.summary: This minor release turns the backup and portability foundation into a real admin export action.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added an admin-only one-click export download in Admin Settings.
Added `POST /api/admin/settings/portability/export`.
Export downloads are gzip-compressed collectZ JSON bundles.
Export bundles include a manifest, app version, generation timestamp, portable database tables, upload-storage manifest, restore guidance, and redaction metadata.
Secret-looking fields and secret-looking URL parameters are recursively redacted before the export is written.
The existing portability readback now reports manual export capability.
Admin Settings shows the last successful export download for the current browser session.

releases.title: collectZ v3.14.5
releases.summary: This patch adds practical edition and variant editing to Library drawers using the reusable collectible trait contract.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added a compact Edition drawer section for Library records.
Edition controls now change by media type instead of using one generic variant field.
Books support edition, printing, publisher line, ARC, first edition, limited release, and numbered-run details.
Comics support variant cover, printing, run context, newsstand/direct edition, limited release, and numbered-run details.
Games support platform, region, release line, collector edition, limited release, and promo/demo state.
Movies, TV, and Audio support package, release, pressing, screener/promo, and limited-release style details.
Saved edition details appear through existing collectible trait readback and compact trait pills.

releases.title: collectZ v3.14.4
releases.summary: This patch adds manual relationship workflows for linking related records without duplicating them across libraries.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added a scoped `object_relationships` persistence table and migration.
Added authenticated relationship APIs for searching link targets, listing relationships for a record, saving links, and unlinking archived relationships.
Added a compact `Related` drawer section for Library, Art, and Collectibles records.
Relationship types include includes, part of, included with, companion to, purchased with, and event acquired with.
Relationship readback shows the related record title, record family, optional subtitle context, and optional note from both sides.

releases.title: collectZ v3.14.3
releases.summary: This patch adds owner-level proof and provenance editing for collectible-like records.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added a compact Proof editor to Library, Art, and Collectibles detail drawers.
Proof details are stored through the reusable trait API as the `provenance` family.
Supported fields include proof type, issuer/source, certificate number, acquired-from/source context, evidence date, reference, and notes.
Saved proof details appear through existing collectible trait readback and trait pills.
Reference readback shows that a reference exists without echoing raw local path-style values in the drawer.

releases.title: collectZ v3.14.2
releases.summary: This patch adds the first editable workflow on top of reusable collectible traits: manual grading details for supported collection records.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added a compact grading editor to Library, Art, and Collectibles detail drawers.
Grading details are stored through the reusable trait API as the `graded` family.
Supported fields include grader, grade, certificate number, grading date, and slab notes.
Saved grading details now appear through existing collectible trait readback and trait pills.
The editor remains drawer-based so ordinary add/edit flows stay lightweight.

releases.title: collectZ v3.14.1
releases.summary: This patch continues the reusable collectible traits line by adding the backend persistence contract that future grading, COA/provenance, bundle, and edition editors can build on.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added migration `107` for `collectible_trait_records`.
Added scoped persisted trait APIs under `/api/collectible-traits/{ownerType}/{ownerId}`.
Supported trait owners: media, Art, and Collectibles.
Persisted traits now merge into existing `collectible_traits` readback before derived compatibility traits with the same key.
Expanded the OpenAPI trait contract for stored traits and future families such as graded, bundle, and provenance.
Added personal access token scope mapping for the trait API as collection data.

releases.title: collectZ v3.14.0
releases.summary: This minor release starts the reusable collectible traits milestone with a shared readback contract for special item details that already exist across the app.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added backend `collectible_traits` summaries for media, Art, and Collectibles responses.
Derived shared traits from existing data instead of adding new required fields.
Covers signed/autographed, numbered/limited, certificate/proof, event-acquired/exclusive, and edition/variant readback where current data supports it.
Added compact trait chips and drawer readback in Library, Art, and Collectibles surfaces.
Added an OpenAPI `CollectibleTrait` schema and documented optional trait arrays on concrete response schemas.
Promoted the selected backlog task into the roadmap as `3.14.0`.

releases.title: collectZ v3.13.0
releases.summary: This minor release starts the backup/export/portability product surface by adding read-only Admin Settings status for database records, uploaded images, provider metadata, restore guidance, and the backup runbook.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added an admin-only `GET /api/admin/settings/portability` readback endpoint.
Added a `Backup and portability` section to Admin Settings.
Shows database reachability with redacted database location details.
Shows uploaded image storage status, file count, and size when local uploads are visible to the backend.
Shows export coverage counts for collection records, wishlist rows, loans, captures, events, libraries, and workspaces.
Shows provider metadata coverage and restore guidance without exposing secret values.
Documented the endpoint in OpenAPI and moved the selected backlog item into the roadmap.

releases.title: collectZ v3.12.17
releases.summary: This patch aligns the frontend Docker build image with the current Vite and ZXing dependency contract by moving the frontend builder from Node 20 to Node 24.

releases.title: collectZ v3.12.16
releases.summary: This patch finishes a small post-Vite dependency hygiene pass by removing stale CRA/Webpack-era frontend package overrides that no longer apply to the current Vite dependency tree.
